findings.evidence: `rg -n "galoisGroupEquiv|signPattern" code/TauCeti` shows only the def at line 249 and sign-pattern lemmas; there is no `galoisGroupEquiv_apply`, `galoisGroupEquiv_symm_apply`, or generator-action theorem for the equivalence.
findings.fix: Add simp-characteristic lemmas such as `(galoisGroupEquiv hroot hindep σ).toAdd = signPattern root σ` and `((galoisGroupEquiv hroot hindep).symm (Multiplicative.ofAdd ε)) (gen root i) = (-1) ^ (ε i).val * gen root i`, with appropriate `@[simp]` where they give the normal form.
findings.issue: `galoisGroupEquiv` has no public apply/symm characterization, so downstream code cannot use the advertised sign-change isomorphism without unfolding the `MulEquiv.ofBijective` body.

findings.evidence: Grep hits: lines 50, 73, 95, 100, and 107 are public theorems used to prove `isSplittingField`/`isGalois`, while roadmap `Multiquadratic/Targets.lean` lines 48-65 exposes only the Galois instance target and the group equivalence.
findings.fix: Make proof-only helpers `private` or move them into a private section; keep public only the defining polynomial/generator if they are intentionally part of the characteristic API, plus their documented simp lemmas.
findings.issue: Proof-internal lemmas and definitions (`splits_X_sq_sub_C`, `adjoin_gen_eq_top`, `mqPoly_ne_zero`, `splits_mqPoly`, `isAlgebraic_gen`) are exported as public API even though the roadmap target only needs `isGalois`, `signHom`/sign data, and `galoisGroupEquiv`.

summary: The main equivalence is exposed without the characteristic API that makes it usable as the sign-change isomorphism, and several proof-only helpers are unnecessarily public.
